title: September is the best time to visit Beijing

vocabulary.hint: Focus on 'lǚyóu' to remember it means 'to travel'.
Focus on 'juédé' to remember it means 'to think, to feel'.
Focus on 'zuì' to remember it means 'most'.
Focus on 'wèishéme' to remember it means 'why'.
Focus on 'yě' to remember it means 'also, too'.
Focus on 'yùndòng' to remember it means 'sport, exercise'.
Focus on 'tī zúqiú' to remember it means 'to play football'.
Focus on 'yìqǐ' to remember it means 'together'.
Focus on 'yào' to remember it means 'to want, would like'.
Focus on 'xīn' to remember it means 'new'.
Focus on 'tā' to remember it means 'it'.
Focus on 'yǎnjīng' to remember it means 'eye'.

grammar.explanation: 要 is used before a verb to indicate a desire or necessity ('to want to', 'need to').

examples.english: I want to go to Beijing to travel.

grammar.explanation: 最 is used before an adjective or a verb to indicate the superlative degree ('most').

examples.english: The weather in Beijing is the best in September.

title: I get up at six every day

Focus on 'xiūxi' to remember it means 'to rest'.
Focus on 'máng' to remember it means 'busy'.
Focus on 'shíjiān' to remember it means 'time'.

grammar.explanation: 每 means 'every' or 'each'. It is often used with a measure word.

examples.english: I get up at six every day.

grammar.explanation: Used to ask about the degree of something (how tall, how old, etc.).

examples.english: How tall are you?

vocabulary.hint: Focus on 'shǒubiǎo' to remember it means 'watch'.

Focus on 'zhēn' to remember it means 'really, indeed'.
Focus on 'fěnsè' to remember it means 'pink'.
Focus on 'yánsè' to remember it means 'color'.
Focus on 'zuǒbian' to remember it means 'left side'.
Focus on 'hóngsè' to remember it means 'red'.

grammar.explanation: A '的' phrase is formed by adding '的' to a noun, pronoun, or adjective, acting as a noun.

examples.pinyin: Zhè kuài shǒubiǎo shì wǒ de.
examples.english: This watch is mine.

grammar.explanation: Used after a verb to indicate a short or brief action.

examples.english: Have a look.

title: He recommended me for this job

grammar.explanation: Used to emphasize the person who performed a past action.

examples.pinyin: Zhè běn shū shì gēge mǎi de.
examples.english: This book was bought by my older brother.

grammar.explanation: Used to indicate that an action has already taken place or a state has reached a certain point.

examples.english: I have already taken the medicine.

grammar.explanation: Used to indicate a conclusion or resolution reached based on what has been said.

examples.pinyin: Nǐ jiù mǎi zhè jiàn ba.
examples.english: Then just buy this one.

grammar.explanation: The basic form is 'A + 比 + B + Adjective'.

examples.english: Today is hotter than yesterday.

grammar.explanation: Used to indicate a range in time or space ('from... to...').

examples.english: from Beijing to Shanghai

grammar.explanation: Used to indicate the distance between two places.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ jiā lí xuéxiào hěn jìn.

grammar.explanation: Attached to a verb to indicate that a state is continuing.

examples.english: The door is open.

grammar.point: The adverbs 也 (yě) and 都 (dōu) for total negation
grammar.explanation: Used with '一 + Measure Word + Noun' to indicate complete negation.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ yí ge píngguǒ yě bù xiǎng chī.
examples.english: I don't want to eat even one apple.

title: There were too many questions; I didn't finish

vocabulary.hint: Focus on 'cuò' to remember it means 'wrong, mistaken'.
Focus on 'cóng' to remember it means 'from'.
Focus on 'tiàowǔ' to remember it means 'to dance'.
Focus on 'dì-yī' to remember it means 'first'.
Focus on 'xīwàng' to remember it means 'to hope'.
Focus on 'wèntí' to remember it means 'question, problem'.
Focus on 'huānyíng' to remember it means 'to welcome'.
Focus on 'shàngbān' to remember it means 'to go to work'.
Focus on 'dǒng' to remember it means 'to understand'.
Focus on 'wán' to remember it means 'to finish, end'.
Focus on 'tí' to remember it means 'question (in test)'.

grammar.explanation: A verb or adjective used after a verb to indicate the result of the action (e.g., 完, 懂, 对, 好).

examples.english: I have finished doing the questions.

grammar.explanation: 就 indicates that an action happens early/fast, while 才 indicates it happens late/slow.

examples.pinyin: Tā bā diǎn jiù lái le.
examples.english: He came as early as eight o'clock.

vocabulary.hint: Focus on 'bié' to remember it means 'don't'.

Focus on 'zhèngzài' to remember it means 'in process of'.
Focus on 'ne' to remember it means 'indicating a state'.
Focus on 'bāngzhù' to remember it means 'to help'.
Focus on 'kè' to remember it means 'lesson, class'.

grammar.explanation: Used after a verb to indicate a past experience ('have done').

examples.english: I have seen him before.

grammar.point: The structure '要...了' (yào... le) for imminent action
grammar.explanation: Indicates that an action is about to happen soon.

examples.english: It is going to rain.

grammar.explanation: Subject + 把 + Object + Verb + Other elements. Used when an object is affected by an action.

examples.pinyin: Wǒ bǎ shū fàng zài zhuōzi shang le.
